message:
fix(docs): clarify that actrc doesn't evaluate shell expressions

The previous fix messages and documentation incorrectly suggested adding
`$(id -u):$(id -g)` to ~/.actrc. Since actrc is a plain text file (not
a shell script), these expressions are NOT evaluated - users must put
their actual numeric UID:GID values.

Fixed:
- dsr doctor: Now says "Run 'id' to get your UID:GID" instead of
  showing unexpanded shell variables
- README.md: Shows literal "1000:1000" with note to replace

The "Quick fix" echo command still uses $(id -u) because that command
IS evaluated when run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -146,9 +146,9 @@ For local builds:
 > cp config/actrc.example ~/.actrc
 > ```
 >
-> Or ensure your `~/.actrc` includes:
+> Or add this line to your `~/.actrc` (replace 1000:1000 with your UID:GID from `id`):
 > ```
-> --container-options --user=$(id -u):$(id -g)
+> --container-options --user=1000:1000
 > ```
 >
 > Run `dsr doctor` to verify your configuration.

@@ -595,8 +595,8 @@ EOF
             log_error "  actrc: CRITICAL - --bind without --user flag"
             log_error "         Files created by act will have wrong ownership (UID 1001)"
             log_error "         This WILL break file access for other processes!"
-            fixes+=("Fix actrc: Add to ~/.actrc: --container-options --user=\$(id -u):\$(id -g)")
-            fixes+=("Or run: echo '--container-options --user=$(id -u):$(id -g)' >> ~/.actrc")
+            fixes+=("Fix actrc: Run 'id' to get your UID:GID, then add to ~/.actrc: --container-options --user=UID:GID")
+            fixes+=("Quick fix: echo '--container-options --user=$(id -u):$(id -g)' >> ~/.actrc")
             ((errors++))
         elif $has_bind && $has_user; then
             _add_check "actrc" "ok" ", \"file\": \"$actrc_file\", \"bind\": true, \"user_mapping\": true"
